title: pinus ayacahuite subsp. loudoniana
intro: Pinus ayacahuite subsp. loudoniana, also known as the Mexican white pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its terpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Pinus ayacahuite subsp. loudoniana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Pinus ayacahuite subsp. loudoniana can be made by infusing the needles in hot water or oil, and are often used to treat coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
